title: Challenge Lab: Launch & Manage a Spot EC2 Web Server

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. Otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. Otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed in to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

In this challenge, you are required to launch and configure a Spot Instance in Amazon EC2, set up a basic web server using Apache, and verify cost-saving mechanisms. You must perform the tasks using hints only, without step-by-step instructions. This tests your practical understanding of EC2 Spot Instances and AWS Console navigation.

You're working as a cloud engineer for a startup aiming to reduce cloud infrastructure costs. Your task is to launch a cost-effective EC2 instance using Spot pricing, configure it with basic web server setup, and ensure it's publicly accessible. You'll need to set the right pricing strategy, configure the network and security rules, and validate that the instance is serving content correctly — all without step-by-step guidance.

Launch an EC2 Spot Instance Choose Amazon Linux AMI and t2.micro instance type. Select Proceed without a key pair. Enable public IP and create a security group allowing SSH (port 22) and HTTP (port 80). Select Spot Instance, set maximum price to $0.003, and choose One-time request. Note: If you get an error message stating that your Maximum price is less than the current price shown in the Availability zone and Current Price table, click Edit instance config, set the Maximum price to $0.01, and then click Launch Instances.
Choose Amazon Linux AMI and t2.micro instance type.
Select Proceed without a key pair.
Enable public IP and create a security group allowing SSH (port 22) and HTTP (port 80).
Select Spot Instance, set maximum price to $0.003, and choose One-time request.

Note: If you get an error message stating that your Maximum price is less than the current price shown in the Availability zone and Current Price table, click Edit instance config, set the Maximum price to $0.01, and then click Launch Instances.
You need to create and provide a User Data script that installs a web server and serves a custom HTML message when the EC2 instance launches.
Verify Web Page Access the instance via Public IPv4 and verify the webpage displays.
Access the instance via Public IPv4 and verify the webpage displays.
Review Spot Request Check the Spot Request details like max price and request status.
Check the Spot Request details like max price and request status.
Analyze Pricing & Savings Explore the Spot Savings Summary and Pricing History.
Explore the Spot Savings Summary and Pricing History.
Click Validate after setting up.
Delete resources once validation is complete.
Sign out of the AWS Account
You have successfully completed the challenge.
Click on End Challenge button from the lab console and wait till the process gets completed.
